topics.name: Codes and Standards
subtopics: Fire Protection For Electronic Data Processing
Standard For Wet Chemical Fire Extinguishing
Standard Specification For Hubless Cast Iron

topics.name: Plumbing Terminology
subtopics: Recommended Practice For Conversion To The International System Of Units

questions.prompt: What is the primary purpose of disinfecting drinking water?
questions.explanation_short: Disinfection kills harmful bacteria and pathogens.
questions.explanation_long: The primary purpose of disinfecting drinking water is to kill bacteria. Turbidity guidelines refer to water clarity (Option A). Hardness reduction is done through water softening/ion exchange (Option B). Iron and manganese removal is done through filtration (Option D). Reference: ASPE Vol. 2, Chapter 11

questions.prompt: Mechanical work for 2500 kW may be supervised by a:
questions.explanation_short: 2000 kW and above requires PME supervision only.
questions.explanation_long: According to RA 8495 (Philippine Mechanical Engineering Act of 1998), Section 34: 2000 kW and above requires PME (Professional Mechanical Engineer); 300-2000 kW can be supervised by ME or PME; 100-300 kW can be supervised by CPM, ME, or PME.
questions.source_ref: RA 8495 - Philippine Mechanical Engineering Act of 1998, Sec. 34

questions.prompt: What is the meaning of ANSI?
questions.explanation_short: ANSI is the American National Standards Institute.
questions.explanation_long: ANSI stands for American National Standards Institute. It is a private non-profit organization that oversees the development of voluntary consensus standards for products, services, processes, systems, and personnel in the United States.

questions.prompt: What is the heaviest type of copper pipe which is suitable for underground installation?
questions.explanation_short: Type K is the heaviest copper pipe for underground use.
questions.explanation_long: Type K is the heaviest/thickest copper pipe suitable for underground installation. Type M is the thinnest type for small water supply lines. Type L is lighter than K and available in rigid and flexible form. Type DWV is used for drainage and vent piping. Reference: PDE by Max Fajardo, Chapter 2
questions.source_ref: PDE by Max Fajardo, Chapter 2; Audel Plumbers Pocket Manual, Chapter 15

questions.prompt: What is the ASTM standard specification for Zinc (Hot-dipped Galvanized) Coatings on Iron and Steel?
questions.explanation_short: ASTM A123 is for zinc coatings on iron and steel.
questions.explanation_long: ASTM A123 is the Standard Specification for Zinc (Hot-Dip Galvanized) Coatings on Iron and Steel Products. ASTM F493 is for solvent cements for CPVC plastic pipe. ASTM D3309 is for polybutylene plastic system components. ASTM F714 is for high-density polyethylene (HDPE) pipe.

questions.prompt: What is the specific gravity of seawater?
questions.explanation_short: Seawater has a specific gravity of approximately 1.028.
questions.explanation_long: Seawater has a specific gravity of approximately 1.028 due to dissolved salts. Fresh water has a specific gravity of 1.0. Gasoline is approximately 0.7. Isopropyl alcohol is approximately 0.78.

questions.prompt: What is the NFPA standard for the National Fuel Gas Code?
questions.explanation_short: NFPA 54 is the National Fuel Gas Code.
questions.explanation_long: NFPA 54 is the National Fuel Gas Code. NFPA 13 is for sprinkler system installation. NFPA 14 is for standpipe and hose systems. NFPA 170 is for fire safety and emergency symbols.

questions.prompt: 1 atmosphere (atm) is equal to:
questions.explanation_short: 1 atm equals 101,325 Pa, 14.7 psi, and 760 torr.
questions.explanation_long: Standard atmospheric pressure (1 atm) is equivalent to: 101,325 Pascals (Pa), 14.7 pounds per square inch (psi), 760 millimeters of mercury (mmHg) or 760 torr, and 1.01325 bar.

questions.prompt: What is the chemical symbol for Copper?
questions.explanation_short: Cu is the chemical symbol for Copper.
questions.explanation_long: Cu is the chemical symbol for Copper from its Latin name 'Cuprum'. Ti is Titanium, Sn is Tin, and Co is Cobalt.

questions.prompt: What is the approximate height above floor level of a kitchen sink?
questions.explanation_short: Kitchen sink height is approximately 36 inches.
questions.explanation_long: Kitchen sink height is approximately 36 inches. Lavatory height is approximately 31 inches. Laundry tray is approximately 34 inches. Built-in bathtub is approximately 16 inches. Reference: Audel Plumbers Pocket Manual, Chapter 1

questions.prompt: What is the color coding for Foam Fire Extinguisher?
questions.explanation_short: Foam fire extinguishers are color-coded cream.
questions.explanation_long: Foam fire extinguishers are color-coded cream. Blue indicates dry powder. Black indicates carbon dioxide. Yellow indicates wet chemical.

questions.prompt: According to RA 9514, what is the shape symbol for Class A - Ordinary Combustible fires?
questions.explanation_short: Class A fires are symbolized by a triangle.
questions.explanation_long: Class A (Ordinary Combustible) fires use a triangle symbol. Class B (Flammable Liquids) uses a square. Class C (Live Electrical Equipment) uses a circle. Class K (Commercial Cooking Equipment) uses a hexagon. Reference: RA 9514 (Fire Code of the Philippines)
questions.source_ref: RA 9514 - Fire Code of the Philippines

questions.prompt: According to ASPE, what is the water hardness range (in mg/l as CaCO3) for the 'Very Hard' category?
questions.explanation_short: Very Hard water is over 300 mg/l as CaCO3.
questions.explanation_long: ASPE water hardness categories: Soft (0-75 mg/l as CaCO3), Moderate (76-150 mg/l as CaCO3), Hard (151-300 mg/l as CaCO3), Very Hard (Over 300 mg/l as CaCO3). CaCO3 = Calcium Carbonate.

questions.prompt: Which gas is NOT typically found inside a septic tank?
questions.explanation_short: Hydrochloric acid (muriatic acid) is not found in septic tanks.
questions.explanation_long: Hydrochloric Acid (Muriatic Acid) is NOT found in septic tanks. Gases found in septic tanks include: Carbon Monoxide (CO), Hydrogen Sulfide (H2S), and Sulfur Dioxide (SO2). Reference: PDE by Max Fajardo, Chapter 4

questions.prompt: Which law is also known as the 'Code on Sanitation of the Philippines'?
questions.explanation_short: PD 856 is the Code on Sanitation of the Philippines.
questions.explanation_long: PD 856 is the Code on Sanitation of the Philippines. RA 8749 is the Philippine Clean Air Act of 1999. RA 9275 is the Philippine Clean Water Act. RA 9003 is the Ecological Solid Waste Act.
questions.source_ref: PD 856 - Code on Sanitation of the Philippines

questions.prompt: What is the minimum gauge number of galvanized sheet metal that could be used as a rainwater downspout or conductor to be located on the exterior side of a low-height building?
questions.explanation_short: GA 26 is the minimum gauge for exterior rainwater downspouts.
questions.explanation_long: GA 26 is the minimum gauge number for galvanized sheet metal used as rainwater downspout or conductor on the exterior side of a low-height building. Reference: RNPCP, Section 1102

questions.prompt: What is the water demand (in gpm) for a drinking fountain?
questions.explanation_short: Drinking fountain water demand is 0.75 gpm.
questions.explanation_long: Water demands: Drinking fountain = 0.75 gpm. Lavatory faucet = 2 gpm. Lavatory faucet (self-closing) = 3 gpm. Bathtub faucet, shower head, and laundry tub faucet = 4 gpm. Reference: Audel Plumbers Pocket Manual, Chapter 15

questions.prompt: What is the appropriate hacksaw blade TPI (teeth per inch) for galvanized steel?
questions.explanation_short: 14 TPI is used for galvanized steel.
questions.explanation_long: Hacksaw blade selection: 32 TPI for light gauge band iron and thin wall copper tubing. 24 TPI for angle iron or pipe. 18 TPI for hanger rod, general use. 14 TPI for galvanized steel. Reference: Audel Plumbers Pocket Manual, Chapter 3

questions.prompt: What is the maximum service temperature of Chlorinated Polyvinyl Chloride (CPVC)?
questions.explanation_long: Maximum service temperatures: PVC = 140°F. ABS = 160°F. CPVC = 180-200°F. PVDF (Polyvinylidene Fluoride) = 280°F. Reference: Audel Plumbers Pocket Manual, Chapter 13

questions.prompt: Which of the following is NOT equal to the density of water?
questions.explanation_short: 1 gram/mm³ is incorrect; it should be 1 gram/cm³.
questions.explanation_long: Water density equivalents: 1 gram/cm³ (not mm³), 62.4 lbs/ft³, 1000 kg/m³, 9.81 kN/m³. Note: Option A uses mm³ instead of cm³, making it incorrect.

questions.prompt: Which document outlines the minimum safety standards for plumbing installations?
questions.explanation_short: The National Plumbing Code sets minimum safety and installation standards.
questions.explanation_long: Plumbing installations follow the National Plumbing Code (or local adaptation) to ensure safe materials, sizing, and installation practices.

questions.prompt: What is the primary goal of correct pipe sizing in water supply systems?
questions.explanation_short: Proper sizing ensures fixtures receive the needed flow and pressure.
questions.explanation_long: Pipe sizing balances pressure loss and flow demand so multiple fixtures can operate without pressure drops.

questions.prompt: Which of the following is NOT a gas typically found inside a septic tank?
questions.explanation_short: Hydrochloric acid is a liquid chemical, not a gas produced by decomposition.
questions.explanation_long: Methane, Carbon Monoxide, Hydrogen Sulfide, and Sulfur Dioxide are gases produced by organic decomposition. Hydrochloric acid is a corrosive liquid often used in cleaning but not naturally produced as a gas in the tank.

questions.prompt: For a septic tank inlet compartment exceeding 3.7 meters in length, what specific addition is required?

choices.label: C
choices.text: An additional manhole over the baffle wall

choices.label: D
choices.text: An increase in liquid depth to 1.8m

questions.explanation_short: Length > 3.7m requires an extra manhole over the baffle.
questions.explanation_long: The code mandates that if the first compartment (digestive chamber) exceeds 3.7m in length, an additional manhole must be provided over the baffle wall to facilitate maintenance.

questions.prompt: What is the minimum horizontal distance required between a water supply well and a disposal field?
questions.explanation_short: Wells to disposal fields require 30.5m separation.
questions.explanation_long: While wells need to be 15.2m from a septic tank, the distance increases to 30.5m when measuring from a disposal field due to the higher risk of ground saturation.

questions.prompt: Concrete septic tanks must be protected from corrosion by coating with bituminous coating. How far must this coating extend?

choices.label: D
choices.text: Only the bottom of the tank

questions.explanation_short: Extend at least 101.6 mm below the water line.
questions.explanation_long: The coating must cover all internal areas above the water line and extend at least 101.6 mm below the water line to protect against corrosive gases.

questions.prompt: What is the minimum vertical vertical clearance of the inlet pipe invert above the outlet pipe invert?
questions.explanation_long: Standard design requires the inlet invert to be 2 inches (50.8 mm) higher than the outlet invert to ensure proper gravity flow and storage.

questions.prompt: What is the ideal sludge volume threshold for cleaning a septic tank?
questions.explanation_long: Cleaning is recommended when sludge occupies 1/3 of the tank volume to prevent solids from carrying over into the disposal field.

questions.prompt: What is the minimum diameter allowed for a seepage pit?
questions.explanation_long: The Appendix B of the RNPCP specifies a minimum diameter of 2.2 meters for seepage pits.

questions.prompt: Dosing tanks are required when the quantity of sewage exceeds the disposal capacity of how many lineal meters of line?
questions.explanation_long: A dosing tank is required when sewage exceeds the amount disposable in 152.4 lineal meters of leach line.

questions.prompt: What is the recommended septic tank capacity for a 3-bedroom single-family dwelling?
questions.explanation_long: Table B-2 recommends 1000 gallons (approx. 3785 liters) for a 3-bedroom home.

questions.prompt: Inlet and outlet pipes or baffles must extend how far below the liquid surface?
questions.explanation_long: This extension ensures that the scum layer is not disturbed and solids do not flow out directly.

questions.prompt: Which type of bacteria functions in the absence of free oxygen?
questions.explanation_long: Anaerobic bacteria thrive in environments without free oxygen, which is the primary mode of decomposition in a septic tank.

questions.prompt: What is the minimum horizontal distance required between a septic tank and a building?
questions.explanation_long: The code requires a 1.5-meter clearance between the septic tank and any building structure.

questions.prompt: Which material is NOT allowed for constructing a septic tank?
questions.explanation_short: Wood is subject to rot and leakage.
questions.explanation_long: Septic tanks must be watertight and durable (concrete, steel, plastic). Wood is not a permitted material due to decay.
questions.source_ref: WEEK 1.pdf | General Knowledge (Implied in materials list)

questions.prompt: What is the primary characteristic of an Imhoff Tank?

choices.label: A
choices.text: It has a single chamber for all processes

choices.label: C
choices.text: It has an upper sedimentation chamber and a lower digestion chamber

choices.label: D
choices.text: It requires a motor for operation

choices.label: E
choices.text: It is designed for chemical waste

questions.explanation_short: Two chambers: upper sedimentation, lower digestion.
questions.explanation_long: The Imhoff tank is a specific two-story tank design where sedimentation occurs in the upper level and digestion in the lower level.

questions.prompt: For effective decomposition, the liquid content depth in a septic tank should be at least:
questions.explanation_long: A depth of 1.2 meters is considered necessary for effective organic decomposition.

questions.prompt: If a septic tank has two compartments, what is the minimum capacity of the digestive (inlet) compartment?
questions.explanation_long: The inlet compartment handles the bulk of the digestion and must be at least 2/3 of the total tank volume.

questions.prompt: The maximum length of an individual drain line in a disposal field is:
questions.explanation_long: Limiting the length to 30.5m ensures even distribution of effluent.

questions.prompt: What is the minimum inspection hole dimension for a seepage pit?
questions.explanation_long: The inspection hole must be at least 9 inches to allow for proper checking and maintenance.

questions.prompt: An abandoned septic tank must be filled within how many days after connection to a public sewer?
questions.explanation_long: Once connected to a public sewer, the old disposal facility poses a hazard and must be filled within 30 days.

questions.prompt: No excavation for a leach line shall extend within what distance of the water table?
questions.explanation_long: A 1.5-meter separation is critical to prevent contamination of the groundwater table.

questions.prompt: What is the minimum required manhole dimension for a septic tank access?
questions.explanation_long: Access manholes must be at least 508 mm in minimum dimension to allow entry for cleaning.

questions.prompt: The minimum horizontal distance between a seepage pit and a disposal field is:
questions.explanation_long: To prevent saturation overlap, a minimum clear horizontal distance of 1.5 meters is required between these units.

questions.prompt: Which soil type has the maximum absorption capacity of 203.7 L/sq.m?
questions.explanation_long: Coarse sand or gravel has the highest absorption rate among typical soils listed in Table B-4.

questions.prompt: The freeboard or air space of a septic tank is measured from:

choices.label: A
choices.text: The bottom of the tank to the liquid level

choices.label: B
choices.text: The liquid level line to the inside top of the tank

choices.label: C
choices.text: The inlet invert to the outlet invert

choices.label: D
choices.text: The top of the tank to the ground surface

choices.label: E
choices.text: The baffle bottom to the tank floor

questions.explanation_long: Freeboard is the air space reserve above the working liquid level, measured up to the inside ceiling of the tank.

questions.prompt: In a septic tank, the 'scum' layer consists primarily of:

choices.label: A
choices.text: Heavy solids that settle to the bottom

choices.label: C
choices.text: Fats, oils, and grease that float

questions.explanation_long: Scum is the lighter material (grease, fats) that floats on top of the liquid, while sludge is the heavier solid that settles to the bottom.

questions.prompt: Water hammer is caused by:
questions.explanation_short: Water hammer results from sudden stoppage of flow.
questions.explanation_long: When moving water is abruptly stopped, a shock wave forms, known as water hammer.

questions.prompt: Which device is used to prevent water hammer?
questions.explanation_long: Air chambers (or arrestors) absorb the shock wave that causes water hammer.

questions.prompt: Static pressure is defined as:

choices.label: B
choices.text: Pressure when water is at rest

choices.label: C
choices.text: Loss of pressure due to friction

questions.explanation_long: Static pressure exists when the water is not moving.

questions.prompt: Dynamic pressure is:
questions.explanation_long: Dynamic pressure is measured while water is in motion.

questions.prompt: Pressure (psi) can be calculated from head (ft) by:
questions.explanation_long: Each foot of head equals 0.433 psi.

questions.prompt: Head refers to:

choices.label: B
choices.text: Vertical distance between water level and outlet

questions.explanation_long: Head is the vertical distance that provides water pressure.

questions.prompt: Friction loss depends on:
questions.explanation_short: Friction loss increases with length and smaller diameter.
questions.explanation_long: Longer pipes and smaller diameters create more friction loss.

questions.prompt: Residual pressure is:
questions.explanation_long: Residual pressure is the remaining pressure while water is flowing.

questions.prompt: What causes low water pressure on upper floors?
questions.explanation_long: Pressure decreases with elevation, leading to lower pressure on upper floors.

questions.prompt: Minimum residual pressure at a fire hydrant is:
questions.explanation_long: Fire hydrants must maintain at least 20 psi while flowing.

questions.prompt: Standard fire hydrant flow is:
questions.explanation_long: Fire hydrants are typically required to supply 1,000 gpm.

questions.prompt: Standard flow duration for hydrants is:
questions.explanation_short: Hydrants should supply 1,000 gpm for 2 hours.
questions.explanation_long: The requirement includes sustaining flow for at least 2 hours.

questions.prompt: Maximum distance between hydrants in urban areas is:
questions.explanation_long: Urban spacing is typically limited to 150 meters.

questions.prompt: Minimum size of hydrant branch line is:
questions.explanation_long: Hydrant branch lines must be at least 6 inches in diameter.

questions.prompt: Fire hydrants are usually painted:
questions.explanation_short: Hydrants are bright red or yellow.
questions.explanation_long: Hydrants are painted in bright, visible colors.

questions.prompt: Normal pressure range in public water mains is:
questions.explanation_long: Typical public water main pressures are maintained between 40 and 60 psi.

questions.prompt: Which system is used to supply water directly from the main without storage?
questions.explanation_long: In an upfeed system, water is supplied directly from the main without storage tanks.

questions.prompt: Which system uses elevated storage tanks to supply water by gravity?
questions.explanation_long: Downfeed systems store water in elevated tanks and deliver it by gravity.

questions.prompt: Which system combines both direct supply and storage?
questions.explanation_short: Combined uses both direct and storage.
questions.explanation_long: Combined systems use both direct supply and storage tanks.

questions.prompt: Which plumbing system is best for high-rise buildings?
questions.explanation_long: High-rise buildings typically use downfeed systems with storage tanks.

questions.prompt: Which system uses pumps to boost water to upper floors?
questions.explanation_long: In upfeed systems, pumps are required to lift water to higher floors.

questions.prompt: Which system ensures continuous water supply even during main failure?
questions.explanation_long: Downfeed systems have storage tanks that can supply water even if the main fails.

questions.prompt: Which system requires the least initial cost?
questions.explanation_long: Upfeed systems are simpler and require less initial cost compared to storage-based systems.

questions.prompt: Which system is most reliable during low pressure in public mains?
questions.explanation_long: Downfeed systems use stored water, making them reliable during low main pressure.

questions.prompt: Which system is most energy intensive?
questions.explanation_long: Upfeed systems require pumps for distribution, making them more energy intensive.

questions.prompt: Which system provides the most stable pressure to fixtures?
questions.explanation_long: Downfeed systems maintain consistent pressure through gravity-fed storage.

questions.prompt: In roughing-in for a lavatory with a 1-1/2 inch drain, what is the maximum distance allowed between the fixture trap and the vent?
questions.explanation_long: For a 38mm (1-1/2 inch) trap arm, the maximum distance to the vent is 1.07 meters or 3 feet 6 inches.

questions.prompt: What type of vent is used to provide circulation of air between the drainage and vent systems, typically connecting the soil stack and vent stack?
questions.explanation_long: A Yoke Vent acts as a relief vent, connecting the soil stack and vent stack at specified intervals (usually every 5 floors) to prevent pressure buildup.

questions.prompt: An additional cleanout is required on a horizontal line if the aggregate offset angle exceeds:
questions.explanation_long: If the total sum of angles in a run exceeds 135 degrees, an additional cleanout is required to ensure a snake can pass through.

questions.prompt: What is the maximum number of water closets allowed on any circuit or loop vent?
questions.explanation_long: The code limits a battery of fixtures on a circuit or loop vent to a maximum of 8 water closets or other fixtures.

questions.prompt: If a drainage pipe 203mm (8 inches) or larger cannot achieve 2% slope due to structural features, what is the minimum allowable slope with approval?
questions.explanation_long: For pipes 203mm or larger, the slope may be reduced to 0.5% (5.3 mm/m) if 2% is impractical and approved by the Administrative Authority.

questions.prompt: What is the Maximum Fixture Unit loading for a 4-inch (102mm) horizontal drainage pipe?
questions.explanation_long: According to Table 7-5, a 102mm (4-inch) horizontal drain can carry up to 216 DFU. A vertical stack of the same size can carry 256 DFU.

questions.prompt: A vent opening must terminate not less than how many meters away from any openable window?
questions.explanation_long: The vent must be at least 3.0 meters away from a window, or extend at least 0.9 meters above it.

questions.prompt: Sterilizers and similar equipment used for sterile materials must be drained through a(n):
questions.explanation_long: Sterile equipment requires a full Air Gap to prevent any possibility of backflow contamination. Air breaks are insufficient.

questions.prompt: Clothes washers in groups of 3 or more shall be rated at how many fixture units each for the common waste pipe?
questions.explanation_long: For batteries of 3 or more washers, they are rated at 6 fixture units each to account for the heavy surge of discharge.

questions.prompt: Interceptors for laundry equipment must prevent the passage of solids with a dimension of:
questions.explanation_long: Laundry interceptors must catch solids like strings, buttons, or rags that are 12.7 mm or larger.

questions.prompt: The vertical distance between the top dip and the crown weir of a trap is called the:
questions.explanation_long: The trap seal is the water column held in the trap, measured from the top dip to the crown weir.

questions.prompt: No building sewer shall be smaller than what diameter?
questions.explanation_long: The code mandates that the building sewer (the line outside the building) must not be smaller than 150 mm diameter.

questions.prompt: A discharge capacity of 0.50 to 0.95 Liters per second (Lps) constitutes how many Fixture Units?
questions.explanation_long: According to Table 7-3 (Discharge Capacity), a flow of 0.50 to 0.95 Lps is equivalent to 2 Fixture Units.

questions.prompt: For continuous flow from a sump pump, every 0.063 L/s of flow is allowed how many fixture units?
questions.explanation_long: For continuous flow devices like pumps or AC equipment, 2 fixture units are assigned for every 0.063 L/s (approx 1 GPM) of flow.

questions.prompt: The size of a yoke vent shall not be less in diameter than:

choices.label: C
choices.text: The smaller of the soil stack or vent stack

choices.label: D
choices.text: The larger of the soil stack or vent stack

questions.explanation_short: The smaller of the soil stack or vent stack.
questions.explanation_long: Code specifies the yoke vent size must be at least equal to the smaller of the two stacks it connects.

questions.prompt: Horizontal drainage lines connecting to other horizontal lines shall enter through which fitting?
questions.explanation_long: To ensure smooth flow, horizontal-to-horizontal connections must use a 45-degree Wye or a Combination Wye and 1/8 Bend. Sanitary Tees are prohibited in this orientation.

questions.prompt: Which type of siphonage is created when a rapid flow of water passes through a pipe, creating a vacuum?
questions.explanation_long: Direct siphonage (self-siphonage) happens in the fixture's own trap. Indirect/Momentum siphonage is caused by flow in the pipe passing the trap.

questions.prompt: A food waste disposer shall be connected to a trap that also serves a sink. True or False?

choices.label: B
choices.text: False, it requires a separate trap.

choices.label: C
choices.text: True, if the sink is double compartment.

choices.label: D
choices.text: False, unless it is a residential sink.

choices.label: E
choices.text: Allowed if using a grease trap.

questions.explanation_short: False. It requires a separate trap.
questions.explanation_long: No food waste disposer shall be installed on a set of sinks served by a single trap; it must have its own separate trap.

questions.prompt: Sumps for 'public use' occupancy must be provided with:
questions.explanation_long: For public use, redundancy is required. Dual pumps must be arranged to function independently in case of failure.

questions.prompt: PEX pipes should be supported at least every:
questions.explanation_long: Due to its flexibility, PEX requires frequent support, specifically every 32 inches according to the source.

questions.prompt: Jointed plastic pipe should be subjected to a pressure test after drying for at least:
questions.explanation_long: Wait for about 2 hours before testing the line under pressure to allow the solvent cement to set initially.

questions.prompt: Cleanouts 51mm (2 inches) or less in diameter require a front clearance of at least:
questions.explanation_long: Smaller cleanouts (51mm or less) require less clearance (305mm) compared to larger ones (450mm).

questions.prompt: What is the Drainage Fixture Unit (DFU) value for a public flushometer valve water closet?
questions.explanation_long: A public water closet with a flushometer valve is rated at 6 DFU. (Private is 4 DFU).

questions.prompt: What is the minimum inlet pipe size for a septic tank?
questions.explanation_long: According to the Septic Tank Construction Diagram, the minimum sewer inlet diameter is 104.6 mm.

questions.prompt: Which material is prohibited for use as a urinal tailpiece?
questions.explanation_long: Urine is corrosive to brass and copper; therefore, the code prohibits their use for urinal tailpieces or traps.

questions.prompt: Which ASTM standard covers the specification for 'Seamless Copper Water Tube'?
questions.explanation_long: ASTM B88 is the standard specification for seamless copper water tubes (Types K, L, M). ASTM A74 is for Cast Iron.

questions.prompt: Which type of valve is best suited for throttling flow?
questions.explanation_long: Globe valves are designed for regulating (throttling) flow, whereas Gate valves are designed for on/off service only.

questions.prompt: According to the National Building Code (Rule X), what is the color code for Steam (High Pressure) piping?
questions.explanation_long: NBC Rule X designates White for High Pressure Steam and Buff for Exhaust Systems.

questions.prompt: What is the melting point of silver solder?
questions.explanation_long: Silver solder has a much higher melting point than soft solder, typically around 1500F to 1750F.

questions.prompt: Which tool is used to install or extract 1-inch through 2-inch nipples without damaging the threads?
questions.explanation_long: An Internal Wrench grips the inside of the nipple, preventing damage to the external threads during installation.

questions.prompt: What is the chemical name of the material commonly known as Teflon tape?
questions.explanation_long: Teflon is the brand name for Polytetrafluoroethylene (PTFE), used for sealing pipe threads.

questions.prompt: Which type of Cast Iron pipe is recommended when the length needed is more than 150 cm and cutting is not necessary?
questions.explanation_long: Single hub pipe is recommended for long runs (over 150 cm) where cutting is not necessary. Double hub is used when shorter lengths need to be cut to create two hubbed ends.

questions.prompt: What is the maximum service temperature for Chlorinated Polyvinyl Chloride (CPVC)?
questions.explanation_long: CPVC can handle higher temperatures than PVC (140F). Sources cite CPVC max service temp around 180F-200F.

questions.prompt: Which color stripe identifies Type DWV copper tube?
questions.explanation_long: K=Green, L=Blue, M=Red, DWV=Yellow.

questions.prompt: Brass valves should not be used for operating temperatures exceeding:
questions.explanation_long: Brass valves are generally limited to temperatures below 450 F (approx 232 C).

questions.prompt: What size hanger rod is required for a 5-inch to 8-inch pipe?
questions.explanation_long: According to UPC Table 313.6 (cited in Source), 5"-8" pipes require a 1/2" rod.

questions.prompt: What is the minimum weight of a caulking ferrule for a 4-inch pipe?
questions.explanation_long: RNPCP specifies the minimum weight for a 102mm (4-inch) caulking ferrule is 1.132 kg.

questions.prompt: A tool used to flare soft copper tubing is called a:
questions.explanation_long: A flaring tool expands the end of the tube into a funnel shape (45 degrees usually) to accept a flare nut.

questions.prompt: What is the approximate length of thread for a 1-inch pipe?
questions.explanation_long: According to the threading table (Week 1 Q#28), a 1-inch pipe has an approximate thread length of 7/8 inch.

questions.prompt: Which fitting is used to close the end of a pipe that has male threads?
questions.explanation_long: A Cap goes over the pipe end (female threads). A Plug goes into a fitting (male threads).

questions.prompt: Hard drawn copper tubing must be marked with a continuous colored stripe at least how wide?
questions.explanation_long: The code requires the colored stripe (Green, Blue, Red, Yellow) to be at least 6.4 mm wide.

questions.prompt: Which valve is an adaptation of the ball valve but uses a cylindrical or tapered stopper?
questions.explanation_long: A Plug Valve uses a tapered or cylindrical plug to stop flow, similar in concept to a ball valve.

questions.prompt: What is the color of a sprinkler glass bulb rated for 135 degrees Celsius?
questions.explanation_long: Source materials list orange for a 135°F (57°C) bulb rating; the prompt likely carries a Celsius/Fahrenheit typo, so the answer follows the source key.
questions.source_ref: WEEK 7.pdf | Q#17 (Note: Source typo likely, C vs F)

questions.prompt: Which material is 'transite pipe' made of?
questions.explanation_long: Transite is a trade name for Asbestos Cement pipe, which is now largely discontinued due to health risks.

questions.prompt: A tool used for dressing or working sheet lead is called:
questions.explanation_long: A Turn Pin is made of boxwood or lignum vitae and is used for flaring or dressing lead pipes.

questions.prompt: The letters 'SDR' on a plastic pipe stand for:
questions.explanation_long: SDR is the ratio of the outside diameter to the wall thickness.

questions.prompt: Which type of valve uses a flat closure element that slides into the flow stream?
questions.explanation_long: A gate valve operates by lifting a gate (flat closure) out of the path of the fluid.

questions.prompt: A pipe fitting used to join two pipes of different sizes, threaded inside and out, is called a:
questions.explanation_long: A bushing fits into a larger opening (male thread) and accepts a smaller pipe (female thread).

questions.prompt: Which type of water meter is most common for residential installations?
questions.explanation_long: Positive displacement meters (like the nutating disc type) are standard for small residential lines due to accuracy at low flows.

questions.prompt: What is the maximum length of a portable ladder?
questions.explanation_long: Single/portable ladders should not exceed 20 feet. Extension ladders can go up to 60 feet.

questions.prompt: According to ASPE, which range describes very hard water?
questions.explanation_short: ASPE classifies water over 300 mg/L as CaCO3 as very hard.
questions.explanation_long: ASPE hardness categories place very hard water above 300 mg/L as calcium carbonate (CaCO3).

questions.prompt: Which of the following is NOT an equivalent expression for the density of water?
questions.explanation_short: Water density is commonly expressed as 1 g/cm3, 1000 kg/m3, 62.4 lb/ft3, or 9.81 kN/m3.
questions.explanation_long: The exception is 1 gram/mm3. Standard equivalents for water are 1 g/cm3, 1000 kg/m3, 62.4 lb/ft3, and 9.81 kN/m3.

questions.prompt: Plumbing fixtures classified as Class 3 are intended for:
questions.explanation_short: Class 3 fixtures are for public or unrestricted use.
questions.explanation_long: The extracted classification notes that Class 3 fixtures are for public use or unrestricted use settings such as schools, terminals, comfort rooms, and similar public occupancies. A cinema fits that class.

questions.prompt: Discharge from a sump pump with 7 gpm of flow is equivalent to how many fixture units?
questions.explanation_short: Continuous flow from a sump pump is rated at 2 fixture units per gpm.
questions.explanation_long: RNPCP Appendix guidance treats continuous flow from sump pumps and similar devices as 2 fixture units for every 1 gpm (0.063 L/s). At 7 gpm, that equals 14 fixture units.

questions.prompt: A pump capable of generating 150 feet of head of gasoline would provide a pressure of about:
questions.explanation_short: Use 150 ft / 2.31 ft per psi, then multiply by gasoline specific gravity 0.70.
questions.explanation_long: Water pressure head converts at about 2.31 ft per psi. For gasoline, multiply by the specific gravity: (150 / 2.31) x 0.70 = about 45.5 psig.

questions.prompt: Which form of precipitation is sometimes called mist and consists of tiny liquid droplets about 0.1 mm to 0.5 mm in diameter?
questions.explanation_short: Drizzle is made of very small liquid droplets, often mist-like in appearance.
questions.explanation_long: Drizzle refers to very small liquid water droplets, usually around 0.1 mm to 0.5 mm in diameter, which makes it finer than ordinary rain.

questions.prompt: When a gutter has a semi-circular bottom, what is the depth of the equivalent rectangular gutter area?
questions.explanation_short: The equivalent rectangular gutter depth is 0.392 times the diameter.
questions.explanation_long: RNPCP gutter sizing references use 0.392 x diameter as the depth of the equivalent rectangular gutter when the actual gutter bottom is semi-circular.

questions.prompt: If a pressure reading is 120 m of water head, what is the equivalent pressure in kPa?
questions.explanation_short: Convert head using 1 m of water head = 9.81 kPa.
questions.explanation_long: Multiply 120 m by 9.81 kPa per meter of water head. That gives 1177.2 kPa, which rounds to 1177 kPa.

questions.prompt: A closed cylindrical container is filled with a fluid that has a specific gravity of 1.7. What pressure does this fluid exert at a depth of 15 m?
questions.explanation_short: Use P = rho x g x h with rho = 1700 kg/m3.
questions.explanation_long: Specific gravity 1.7 means the fluid density is 1700 kg/m3. Using P = rho x g x h gives 1700 x 9.81 x 15 = 250,155 Pa.

questions.prompt: If flow through a 12-inch pipe is 830 gpm, what is the velocity in feet per second?
questions.explanation_short: Use the volumetric flow relation Q = V x A.
questions.explanation_long: Convert 830 gpm to cubic feet per second, compute the cross-sectional area of a 12-inch pipe, and use Q = V x A. The velocity comes out to about 2.35 ft/s.

questions.prompt: What type of pump uses an impeller mounted on a rotating shaft to increase water velocity?
questions.explanation_short: A centrifugal pump raises fluid velocity by spinning an impeller.
questions.explanation_long: Centrifugal pumps use an impeller on a rotating shaft. The impeller imparts velocity to the water, which is then converted into pressure energy.

questions.prompt: Which solder alloy is best for plumbing brazing or soldering joints in this reviewer set?
questions.explanation_short: The keyed answer in the source material is 95% tin and 5% alloy.
questions.explanation_long: This reviewer item keys the best solder choice as 95% tin and 5% alloy, which matches the extracted explanation in the source pages.

questions.prompt: Gaskets must withstand pressure, temperature, and chemical attack. Gaskets normally should be as ____ as possible.
questions.explanation_short: Standard gaskets are thin so they can form an effective seal.
questions.explanation_long: The extracted explanation notes that most standard gaskets are about 1/16 inch or 1/8 inch thick and should be as thin as possible while still creating an effective seal.

questions.prompt: What is the minimum size of the waste pipe and trap in a CW and VS relative to the required pipe size and tailpiece size?
questions.explanation_short: The waste pipe and trap must be at least two pipe sizes larger.
questions.explanation_long: RNPCP Chapter 8 requires each waste pipe and trap in any CW and VS to be at least two pipe sizes larger than the size required by code and at least two pipe sizes larger than any fixture tailpiece or connection.

questions.prompt: What is the fixture unit rating of clothes washers when installed in groups of three or more?
questions.explanation_short: Clothes washers in groups of three or more are rated at 6 fixture units each.
questions.explanation_long: The extracted RNPCP guidance states that clothes washers in groups of three or more shall be rated at six fixture units each for the common horizontal and vertical waste pipe.

questions.prompt: What is the water supply fixture unit (WFSU) value for a public flushometer valve?
questions.explanation_short: RNPCP Table 6-5 lists a public flushometer valve at 10 WFSU.
questions.explanation_long: This reviewer item aligns with RNPCP Table 6-5 on equivalent water supply fixture units, where the keyed value for a public flushometer valve is 10 WFSU.

questions.prompt: What device is located at the bottom of a tank to flush a water closet or similar fixture?
questions.explanation_short: The flush valve is the tank component that releases water into the fixture.
questions.explanation_long: A flush valve is located at the bottom of the tank and opens to discharge water into the water closet or similar fixture for flushing.

questions.prompt: What is the horizontal vent that connects one or more individual back vents with the vent stack or stack vent?
questions.explanation_short: A branch vent ties individual back vents into the main venting system.
questions.explanation_long: A branch vent is the horizontal vent line that connects one or more individual back vents to the vent stack or stack vent.

questions.prompt: Where should a gravity grease interceptor not be installed?

choices.label: A
choices.text: In any part of a building where food is handled

questions.explanation_short: Gravity grease interceptors should not be installed where food is handled.
questions.explanation_long: The cited grease-interceptor guidance states that a gravity grease interceptor shall not be installed in any part of a building where food is handled.

questions.prompt: A dosing tank shall have a capacity equal to what percentage of the interior capacity of the pipe to be dosed at one time?
questions.explanation_short: Dosing tanks are sized at 60% to 75% of the pipe capacity being dosed.
questions.explanation_long: Appendix guidance for disposal fields states that the dosing tank shall have a capacity equal to 60% to 75% of the interior capacity of the pipe to be dosed at one time.

questions.prompt: What valve shall be installed before each water-supplied appliance, slip-joint supply, and nonmetallic fixture or appliance supply piping?
questions.explanation_short: Control gate valves are required ahead of these water-supplied connections.
questions.explanation_long: The referenced water-supply rule states that control gate valves shall be installed before each water-supplied appliance, slip-joint supply, and supply piping for nonmetallic fixtures and appliances.

questions.prompt: Horizontal drainage lines connecting with other horizontal drainage lines shall enter through all of the following except:

choices.label: B
choices.text: Combination wye and one-eighth bend branches

questions.explanation_short: The code permits 45-degree wyes, combination wyes with one-eighth bends, or equivalent sweep fittings.
questions.explanation_long: Horizontal drainage lines entering other horizontal drainage lines must use 45-degree wye branches, combination wye and one-eighth bend branches, or other approved fittings of equivalent sweep. A 60-degree wye branch is the exception.

questions.prompt: What is the minimum net free opening required for the inlet section of a sand interceptor?
questions.explanation_short: A sand interceptor needs at least 0.2 m2 net free opening at the inlet section.
questions.explanation_long: The sand interceptor requirement states that the net free opening of the inlet section shall have a minimum area of 0.2 m2, with at least 610 mm depth under the invert of the outlet pipe.

questions.prompt: Each grease trap shall have an approved water seal of not less than what depth, or the diameter of its outlet, whichever is greater?
questions.explanation_short: The minimum grease-trap water seal is 51 mm.
questions.explanation_long: Each grease trap shall have an approved water seal not less than 51 mm in depth or not less than the diameter of its outlet, whichever is greater.

questions.prompt: At the lowest section of a conductor draining over a catch basin, storm drain, or storm sewer, what stronger material may be used for protection from damage?
questions.explanation_short: The lowest section may be protected with steel pipe or cast iron.
questions.explanation_long: The rule for rainwater conductors allows the lowest section to be protected from damage by connecting a stronger material such as steel pipe or cast iron.

questions.prompt: No building sewer shall be smaller than what diameter, and it shall not be less in size than the building drain?
questions.explanation_short: The minimum size for a building sewer is 150 mm.
questions.explanation_long: The cited house-drain and house-sewer provision states that no building sewer shall be smaller than 150 mm in diameter and it must not be smaller than the building drain.

questions.prompt: Medical gas piping is most often joined by which method?
questions.explanation_short: Medical gas copper piping is commonly joined by silver brazing.
questions.explanation_long: Copper-to-copper joints for medical gas pipework are commonly brazed using a silver-containing brazing filler metal, so silver brazing is the keyed method here.

questions.prompt: Which of the following is the exception to the rule that pipes shall be iron pipe size (IPS) with appropriate weight?
questions.explanation_short: Iron, steel, brass, and copper follow iron pipe size; lead does not.
questions.explanation_long: The material rule states that iron, steel, brass, and copper pipes shall be iron pipe size with appropriate weight. Lead is the exception among the listed choices.

questions.prompt: How high shall a vent stack extend above the roof?
questions.explanation_short: A vent stack shall terminate at least 15 cm above the roof.
questions.explanation_long: Each vent pipe or stack through the roof shall terminate vertically not less than 15 cm above the roof and not less than 0.3 m from any nearby vertical surface.

questions.prompt: How is water pollution that carries disease organisms most commonly corrected?
questions.explanation_short: Chlorination is the standard correction for disease-carrying contamination in water.
questions.explanation_long: The referenced water-quality correction table identifies chlorination as the correction for pollution or contamination in water that carries disease organisms.

questions.prompt: Which term describes a disease that occurs infrequently and irregularly?
questions.explanation_short: A sporadic disease occurs infrequently and irregularly.
questions.explanation_long: Among the standard disease-occurrence terms, sporadic refers to a condition that appears infrequently and irregularly rather than maintaining a baseline pattern or spreading broadly.

questions.prompt: Solid angles are measured in which unit?
questions.explanation_short: A steradian is the SI unit for measuring solid angles.
questions.explanation_long: Plane angles are measured in radians, while solid angles are measured in steradians.

questions.prompt: A value of 2.5 score is equivalent to how many years?
questions.explanation_short: One score is equal to 20 years.
questions.explanation_long: A score means 20 years. Therefore, 2.5 score equals 2.5 x 20 = 50 years.

questions.prompt: Which ASPE term matches this definition? The greatest stress that a material can withstand without permanent deformation after the release f the stress
questions.explanation_long: Elastic limit: The greatest stress that a material can withstand without permanent deformation after the release f the stress

questions.prompt: Which ASPE term matches this definition? The total range of variation permitted; the upper and lower limits between which a dimension must be maintained
questions.explanation_long: Tolerance: The total range of variation permitted; the upper and lower limits between which a dimension must be maintained

questions.prompt: Which ASPE term matches this definition? Plumbing fixtures located together on the same level, generally a water closet, lavatory, and shower or bathtub
questions.explanation_long: Bathroom group Several: plumbing fixtures located together on the same level, generally a water closet, lavatory, and shower or bathtub

questions.prompt: Which ASPE term matches this definition? The point at which any substance has no molecular motion and no heat. It is equivalent to -459.72°F r -273.18°C
questions.explanation_long: Absolute zero: The point at which any substance has no molecular motion and no heat. It is equivalent to -459.72°F r -273.18°C
questions.source_ref: ASPE PLUMBING TERMINOLOGY.pdf | p.1 | Plumbing Terminology

questions.prompt: Which ASPE term matches this definition? Wastewater drainage from equipment, rooms, and other areas that does not contain contaminants considered harmful
questions.explanation_long: Clearwater waste Clear: wastewater drainage from equipment, rooms, and other areas that does not contain contaminants considered harmful

questions.prompt: Which ASPE term matches this definition? The amount a pressure, temperature, liquid level, or differential pressure deviates higher from a set value
questions.explanation_long: Accumulation: The amount a pressure, temperature, liquid level, or differential pressure deviates higher from a set value
questions.source_ref: ASPE PLUMBING TERMINOLOGY.pdf | p.1 | Plumbing Terminology

questions.prompt: Which ASPE term matches this definition? The amount a pressure, temperature, liquid level, or differential pressure deviates lower than a set value
questions.explanation_long: Droop: The amount a pressure, temperature, liquid level, or differential pressure deviates lower than a set value

questions.prompt: Which ASPE term matches this definition? An enclosed passage, primarily vertical, for removal of the gaseous products of combustion to the exterior
questions.explanation_long: Flue: An enclosed passage, primarily vertical, for removal of the gaseous products of combustion to the exterior

questions.prompt: Which ASPE term matches this definition? The temperature of a liquid where the internal vapor pressure is equal to the pressure on the surface f the liquid
questions.explanation_long: Boiling point: The temperature of a liquid where the internal vapor pressure is equal to the pressure on the surface f the liquid

questions.prompt: Which ASPE term matches this definition? An approved pump with a driver, controls, and accessories used to supply water for fire protection service
questions.explanation_long: Fire pump: An approved pump with a driver, controls, and accessories used to supply water for fire protection service

questions.prompt: Which ASPE term matches this definition? The degree of conformity of a measured or calculated value to some recognized standard or specified value
questions.explanation_long: Accuracy: The degree of conformity of a measured or calculated value to some recognized standard or specified value
questions.source_ref: ASPE PLUMBING TERMINOLOGY.pdf | p.23 | Terminology And Abbreviations

questions.prompt: Which ASPE term matches this definition? The unobstructed vertical distance between the end of a pipe and the flood level of a plumbing fixture r receptacle
questions.explanation_long: Air gap: The unobstructed vertical distance between the end of a pipe and the flood level of a plumbing fixture r receptacle
questions.source_ref: ASPE PLUMBING TERMINOLOGY.pdf | p.1 | Plumbing Terminology

questions.prompt: Which ASPE term matches this definition? The temperature at which a fluid gives off flammable vapor in sufficient concentration to form an ignitable mixture
questions.explanation_long: Flash point: The temperature at which a fluid gives off flammable vapor in sufficient concentration to form an ignitable mixture

questions.prompt: Which ASPE term matches this definition? The pressure in a water supply pipe near the water outlet while the faucet or water outlet is fully pen and flowing
questions.explanation_long: Flow pressure: The pressure in a water supply pipe near the water outlet while the faucet or water outlet is fully pen and flowing

questions.prompt: Which ASPE term matches this definition? That conveys corrosive or harmful industrial, chemical, or processed wastes to a separate drainage system
questions.explanation_long: Chemical waste system Piping: that conveys corrosive or harmful industrial, chemical, or processed wastes to a separate drainage system

questions.prompt: Which ASPE term matches this definition? The ability of a chemical compound or material to attack, eat away, and damage materials or human beings
questions.explanation_long: Corrosive: The ability of a chemical compound or material to attack, eat away, and damage materials or human beings

questions.prompt: Which ASPE term matches this definition? The rated percentage of heat produced compared to the actual heat transferred to the medium being heated
questions.explanation_long: Combustion efficiency: The rated percentage of heat produced compared to the actual heat transferred to the medium being heated

questions.prompt: Which ASPE term matches this definition? The portion of a waste stack where the formation of soap suds could create pressure higher than atmospheric pressure
questions.explanation_long: Suds pressure zone: The portion of a waste stack where the formation of soap suds could create pressure higher than atmospheric pressure

questions.prompt: Which ASPE term matches this definition? That the product or material characteristics are physically changed from existing values to preferred SI unit values
questions.explanation_long: Hard These conversions denote: that the product or material characteristics are physically changed from existing values to preferred SI unit values
questions.source_ref: ASPE PLUMBING TERMINOLOGY.pdf | p.24 | Types Of Conversion

questions.prompt: Which ASPE term matches this definition? An opening or fitting in a pipe that can be removed for the purpose of cleaning or examining the interior of the pipe
questions.explanation_long: Cleanout: An opening or fitting in a pipe that can be removed for the purpose of cleaning or examining the interior of the pipe

questions.prompt: Which ASPE term matches this definition? The vertical main pipe of a system of soil, waste, or vent piping extending through one or more stories
questions.explanation_long: Stack: The vertical main pipe of a system of soil, waste, or vent piping extending through one or more stories

questions.prompt: Which ASPE term matches this definition? The depth of water in a fixture trap that prevents the passage of noxious odors but allows the free flow f wastewater
questions.explanation_long: Water seal: The depth of water in a fixture trap that prevents the passage of noxious odors but allows the free flow f wastewater

questions.prompt: Which ASPE term matches this definition? The accumulated waste solids of sewage deposited in tanks, beds, or basins, mixed with water to form a semiliquid mass
questions.explanation_long: Sludge: The accumulated waste solids of sewage deposited in tanks, beds, or basins, mixed with water to form a semiliquid mass

questions.prompt: Which ASPE term matches this definition? The temperature and pressure for a pure substance where the three phases (liquid, solid, and gas) exist in equilibrium
questions.explanation_long: Triple point: The temperature and pressure for a pure substance where the three phases (liquid, solid, and gas) exist in equilibrium

questions.prompt: Which ASPE term matches this definition? The water supply pipe for public or community use, normally under the jurisdiction of the municipality or water company
questions.explanation_long: Water main: The water supply pipe for public or community use, normally under the jurisdiction of the municipality or water company

questions.prompt: Which ASPE term matches this definition? The numerical value that describes the increase in unit length and area of volume as a result of heat
questions.explanation_long: Coefficient of expansion: The numerical value that describes the increase in unit length and area of volume as a result of heat

questions.prompt: Which ASPE term matches this definition? The variation from a specified dimension or design requirement, defining the upper and lower limits
questions.explanation_long: Deviation: The variation from a specified dimension or design requirement, defining the upper and lower limits
questions.source_ref: ASPE PLUMBING TERMINOLOGY.pdf | p.23 | Terminology And Abbreviations

questions.prompt: Which ASPE term matches this definition? The discharge from any fixture, appliance, area, or appurtenance that does not contain fecal matter
questions.explanation_long: Waste: The discharge from any fixture, appliance, area, or appurtenance that does not contain fecal matter

questions.prompt: Which ASPE term matches this definition? The maximum design flow rate calculated by multiplying the connected load with the diversity factor
questions.explanation_long: Peak load: The maximum design flow rate calculated by multiplying the connected load with the diversity factor

questions.prompt: Which ASPE term matches this definition? That part of a valve connecting the valve actuator to the valve body; in some valves, it may also contain the stem packing
questions.explanation_long: Bonnet: That part of a valve connecting the valve actuator to the valve body; in some valves, it may also contain the stem packing

questions.prompt: Which ASPE term matches this definition? The dimensions from the face of the inlet port to the face of the outlet port of a valve r fitting
questions.explanation_long: Facetoface dimensions: The dimensions from the face of the inlet port to the face of the outlet port of a valve r fitting

questions.prompt: Which ASPE term matches this definition? The amount of time that must elapse to produce the most severe design storm. Also called frequency
questions.explanation_long: Return period: The amount of time that must elapse to produce the most severe design storm. Also called frequency

questions.prompt: Which ASPE term matches this definition? The extension of a soil waste stack above the highest horizontal drain connected to the stack extending to the outside air
questions.explanation_long: Stack vent: The extension of a soil waste stack above the highest horizontal drain connected to the stack extending to the outside air

questions.prompt: Which ASPE term matches this definition? The loss of water from soil by both evaporation and transpiration from the plants growing thereon
questions.explanation_long: Evapotranspiration: The loss of water from soil by both evaporation and transpiration from the plants growing thereon

questions.prompt: Which ASPE term matches this definition? The deformation of a material attributed to the pressure or forces acting at ambient temperatures
questions.explanation_long: Cold flow: The deformation of a material attributed to the pressure or forces acting at ambient temperatures

questions.prompt: Which ASPE term matches this definition? The joining of two pipes using a filler metal that has a melting point more than 1,000°F (538 °C)
questions.explanation_long: Brazing: The joining of two pipes using a filler metal that has a melting point more than 1,000°F (538 °C)

questions.prompt: Which ASPE term matches this definition? The area containing a piping arrangement from a septic tank for the purpose of disposing unwanted liquid waste into the soil
questions.explanation_long: Drain field: The area containing a piping arrangement from a septic tank for the purpose of disposing unwanted liquid waste into the soil

questions.prompt: Which ASPE term matches this definition? The measured length of pipe with an additional length to compensate for the friction lost to pipe flow, fittings, and valves
questions.explanation_long: Equivalent run: The measured length of pipe with an additional length to compensate for the friction lost to pipe flow, fittings, and valves

questions.prompt: Which ASPE term matches this definition? The joining of two pipes using a filler metal that has a melting point less than 1,000°F (538°C)
questions.explanation_long: Soldering: The joining of two pipes using a filler metal that has a melting point less than 1,000°F (538°C)

questions.prompt: Which ASPE term matches this definition? The union of two fittings by the insertion of material, accomplished mechanically or chemically
questions.explanation_long: Cement joint: The union of two fittings by the insertion of material, accomplished mechanically or chemically

questions.prompt: Which ASPE term matches this definition? The pressure characteristics at any given temperature of a vapor in equilibrium with its liquid
questions.explanation_long: Vapor pressure: The pressure characteristics at any given temperature of a vapor in equilibrium with its liquid

questions.prompt: Which ASPE term matches this definition? The precise (or direct) conversion to the SI unit value, accurate to a number of decimal places
questions.explanation_long: Exact These conversions denote: the precise (or direct) conversion to the SI unit value, accurate to a number of decimal places
questions.source_ref: ASPE PLUMBING TERMINOLOGY.pdf | p.24 | Types Of Conversion

questions.prompt: Which ASPE term matches this definition? The actual amount of available material necessary to perform the intended function of a device
questions.explanation_long: Output: The actual amount of available material necessary to perform the intended function of a device

questions.prompt: Which ASPE term matches this definition? The maximum design pressure, usually four times normal operating pressure, applied to a piping assembly without causing rupture
questions.explanation_long: Burst pressure: The maximum design pressure, usually four times normal operating pressure, applied to a piping assembly without causing rupture

questions.prompt: Which ASPE term matches this definition? The degree of agreement between a measured value and the true value of a quantity or concern
questions.explanation_long: Accuracy: The degree of agreement between a measured value and the true value of a quantity or concern
questions.source_ref: ASPE PLUMBING TERMINOLOGY.pdf | p.1 | Plumbing Terminology

questions.prompt: Which ASPE term matches this definition? The drain from the trap of a fixture to the junction of that drain with any other drain pipe
questions.explanation_long: Fixture drain: The drain from the trap of a fixture to the junction of that drain with any other drain pipe

questions.prompt: Which ASPE term matches this definition? The maximum vertical depth of liquid that a trap will retain, measured between the crown weir and the top of the dip of the trap
questions.explanation_long: Trap seal: The maximum vertical depth of liquid that a trap will retain, measured between the crown weir and the top of the dip of the trap

questions.prompt: Which ASPE term matches this definition? An organization established for purposes of testing products to approved standards and acceptable to the administrative authority
questions.explanation_long: Approved testing agency: An organization established for purposes of testing products to approved standards and acceptable to the administrative authority

questions.prompt: Which ASPE term matches this definition? The discharge from any fixture, appliance, or appurtenance in connection with a plumbing system that does not receive fecal matter
questions.explanation_long: Liquid waste: The discharge from any fixture, appliance, or appurtenance in connection with a plumbing system that does not receive fecal matter

questions.prompt: Which ASPE term matches this definition? The upper part of a trap where the direction of flow is changed from upward to horizontal
questions.explanation_long: Crown: The upper part of a trap where the direction of flow is changed from upward to horizontal

questions.prompt: Which ASPE term matches this definition? The energy loss of a fluid as it passes through a flow passage. Also called pressure drop
questions.explanation_long: Head loss: The energy loss of a fluid as it passes through a flow passage. Also called pressure drop

questions.prompt: Which ASPE term matches this definition? The effect of exposing material to a specific environment for an extended period of time
questions.explanation_long: Aging: The effect of exposing material to a specific environment for an extended period of time
questions.source_ref: ASPE PLUMBING TERMINOLOGY.pdf | p.1 | Plumbing Terminology

questions.prompt: Which ASPE term matches this definition? The principal artery of a system of continuous piping to which branches may be connected
questions.explanation_long: Main: The principal artery of a system of continuous piping to which branches may be connected

questions.prompt: Which ASPE term matches this definition? The graduation of an instrument with a standard of known accuracy to eliminate variation
questions.explanation_long: Calibration Comparison of: the graduation of an instrument with a standard of known accuracy to eliminate variation

questions.prompt: Which ASPE term matches this definition? An event that occurs at an arbitrary time without synchronization to a referenced clock
questions.explanation_long: Asynchronous: An event that occurs at an arbitrary time without synchronization to a referenced clock

questions.prompt: Which ASPE term matches this definition? The nominal inside diameter of a pipe as commercially designated unless otherwise noted
questions.explanation_long: Diameter: The nominal inside diameter of a pipe as commercially designated unless otherwise noted

questions.prompt: Which ASPE term matches this definition? The purpose for which a building, structure, or portion thereof is utilized or occupied
questions.explanation_long: Occupancy: The purpose for which a building, structure, or portion thereof is utilized or occupied

questions.prompt: Which ASPE term matches this definition? The ability of a substance to produce injurious or lethal effects on a susceptible site
questions.explanation_long: Toxic: The ability of a substance to produce injurious or lethal effects on a susceptible site

questions.prompt: Which ASPE term matches this definition? That portion of a drainage system below the building sewer that cannot drain by gravity
questions.explanation_long: Building subdrain: That portion of a drainage system below the building sewer that cannot drain by gravity

questions.prompt: Which ASPE term matches this definition? Liquid waste containing animal, vegetable, or chemical wastes in suspension or solution
questions.explanation_long: Sewage Any: liquid waste containing animal, vegetable, or chemical wastes in suspension or solution

questions.prompt: Which ASPE term matches this definition? The action by which a liquid is drawn into a void, primarily observed during soldering
questions.explanation_long: Capillary: The action by which a liquid is drawn into a void, primarily observed during soldering

questions.prompt: Which ASPE term matches this definition? The amount of water capable of being heated to the design temperature per unit of time
questions.explanation_long: Recovery rate: The amount of water capable of being heated to the design temperature per unit of time

questions.prompt: Which ASPE term matches this definition? The ability of a material to resist burning when the source of heat has been removed
questions.explanation_long: Selfextinguishing: The ability of a material to resist burning when the source of heat has been removed

questions.prompt: Which ASPE term matches this definition? An angle stop, a valve used to control the water supply, usually to a single fixture
questions.explanation_long: Stop valve Commonly called: an angle stop, a valve used to control the water supply, usually to a single fixture

questions.prompt: Which ASPE term matches this definition? The discharge pipe from any fixture, appliance, or appurtenance in connection with the plumbing system that does not contain fecal matter
questions.explanation_long: Waste pipe: The discharge pipe from any fixture, appliance, or appurtenance in connection with the plumbing system that does not contain fecal matter
